data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cf8fb41c">
    <title>docprose: port initial rules to Vale styles</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The DDx prose checker needs Vale-backed rules for unsupported claims, AI-slop polish, filler, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token cost, and vocabulary. Without tuned rules, `ddx doc prose` cannot improve documentation clarity or token cost reliably.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:85-107 defines the initial rule pack and implementation notes, but the Vale style files and corpus golden cases are not implemented.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Rule semantics are high-judgment work. The bead must balance catching common model-generated prose against preserving precise technical text, commands, IDs, tables, and legitimate density.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Vale style files for the initial DDx rule families.
- Add corpus golden cases for unsupported claims, AI slop, filler transitions, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token-cost candidates, and vocabulary substitution.
- Use Vale scopes/token ignores for Markdown structure.
- Keep raw Vale messages short and leave DDx rationale/suggested edits to normalization.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not switch `ddx doc prose` to Vale yet.
- Do not wire workflow hooks.
- Do not make findings blocking.

DEPS
Depends on the corpus harness so rule behavior is measured against positives and negatives.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestDocProseValeRules_UnsupportedClaim` covers `prose.claim.unsupported`.
2. `TestDocProseValeRules_AISlop` covers `prose.ai_slop.polish`.
3. `TestDocProseValeRules_TokenCost` covers `prose.cost.filler`.
4. `TestDocProseValeRules_StructureIgnores` proves code spans, fenced blocks, paths, commands, frontmatter, and DDx IDs are not flagged unless explicitly positive.
5. Vale 3.13.0 validates the packaged rule files.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/... ./internal/checks/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docprose, area:checks, kind:implementation, prose-quality, adr:025, spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration, tier:smart</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" base-rev="70018bfad16404cf2344595c0fb57186f67312dc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T043546-82590e3d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" path="docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md">Plan: Prose Quality Integration with Vale</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Implemented and committed as `e52fbfa50` (`feat: port initial prose rules to Vale styles [ddx-cf8fb41c]`).

What changed:
- Aligned the embedded technical prose checker with the new rule IDs and Markdown structure ignores in [cli/internal/docprose/checker.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf8fb41c-20260512T043546-82590e3d/cli/internal/docprose/checker.go).
- Updated the packaged rule metadata in [library/checks/prose-quality/rules/technical.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf8fb41c-20260512T043546-82590e3d/library/checks/prose-quality/rules/technical.yaml).
- Replaced the DDx Vale style placeholders with scoped rule skeletons under [library/checks/prose-quality/styles/DDx/](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf8fb41c-20260512T043546-82590e3d/library/checks/prose-quality/styles/DDx/).
- Added and updated corpus cases, including the new fenced-block negative case in [cli/internal/docprose/testdata/corpus/negative/markdown-structure/07-fenced-block/input.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf8fb41c-20260512T043546-82590e3d/cli/internal/docprose/testdata/corpus/negative/markdown-structure/07-fenced-block/input.md).
- Updated the docprose fixture goldens in [cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/technical/generic-ai/findings.golden.json](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf8fb41c-20260512T043546-82590e3d/cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/technical/generic-ai/findings.golden.json).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/... ./internal/checks/...`
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestDocProse|TestProseQuality|TestBeadReview'`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
